task_completion.description: Build at least two interior partition walls and place at least two access doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, there should be at least two new interior walls (thick lines) dividing the space, and at least two door symbols (arcs showing the door swing) placed within the walls.

task_completion.description: Create rooms with distinct floor colors or textures for at least two rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D preview pane, there should be at least two distinct room areas defined with different floor colors or textures (e.g., differentiating the public retail area from the back-of-house area).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Service Counter and Self-Service Packing areas with required desks, computers, tables, and seating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The public zone should contain a service counter with at least 3 desks/counters, 3 computers/registers, and 3 stools/chairs. Nearby, a packing area should have at least 2 high tables and 2 stools. These can be seen in the 3D view or the furniture list panel.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Retail Display and Back-of-House Sorting areas with required shelving and sorting tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The retail area should have at least 3 shelving units. The back-of-house area should contain at least 4 large sorting tables and 8 large shelving units. Verify by looking at the 3D preview or counting items in the left-hand furniture list.

task_completion.description: Place at least 45 total furniture items across all zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The furniture list panel on the left side of the screen should be heavily populated (showing a long list or scrollbar), and the 2D/3D views should look densely furnished, indicating at least 45 items have been placed.

task_completion.description: Document clearances by placing at least three dimension lines in the public area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, there should be at least three dimension lines (lines with measurement text, such as '36 in' or '1 m') placed in the public retail/queue area.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window with the 2D plan, 3D view, and furniture catalog, rather than a terminal window or text editor modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: Furniture, walls, and rooms are genuinely placed and rendered in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D preview pane should visually render the placed walls, doors, floors, and furniture items, confirming they were properly added to the scene and not just textually injected.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file in its current location.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application title bar should show 'shipping_center_starter.sh3d' without an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk), or the agent should be seen clicking 'Save' or using the Ctrl+S shortcut.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The verifier strictly enforces the following cumulative minimum counts: >= 9 total surfaces (tables/desks), >= 11 shelves, >= 3 computers, >= 5 chairs, >= 2 new walls, >= 2 new doors, >= 2 floor zones (rooms with distinct floor colors), >= 3 dimension lines, and >= 45 total furniture items. The total score is 100 points, and the passing threshold is 70 points.
